records.ety: [AS. ængel, engel, influenced by OF. angele, angle, F. ange. Both the AS. and the OF. words are from L. angelus, Gr. 'a`ggelos messenger, a messenger of God, an angel.]

quotes: The dear good angel of the Spring, The nightingale.

defs: A spiritual, celestial being, superior to man in power and intelligence. In the Scriptures the angels appear as God's messengers.
quotes: O, welcome, pure-eyed Faith, white-handed Hope, Thou hovering angel, girt with golden wings.

defs: One of a class of “fallen angels;” an evil spirit; as, the devil and his angels.
A minister or pastor of a church, as in the Seven Asiatic churches.
quotes: Unto the angel of the church of Ephesus write.

defs: An appellation given to a person supposed to be of angelic goodness or loveliness; a darling.
quotes: When pain and anguish wring the brow, A ministering angel thou.

defs: An ancient gold coin of England, bearing the figure of the archangel Michael. It varied in value from 6s. 8d. to 10s.
notes: ☞ Angel is sometimes used adjectively; as, angel grace; angel whiteness.
